description_long: This is a vintage-style electronic keyboard synthesizer, featuring a full set of black and white piano keys on its front face. The top panel is equipped with numerous rotary knobs, sliders, and buttons, likely for sound modulation and control, as well as a small display and labeled sections for different functions. The casing is constructed from a material with a worn, reddish-brown finish, giving it a rugged, well-used appearance. The rear side includes various input/output ports and a visible power cable with a UK-style plug. The surface shows signs of heavy use, with scratches and discoloration, especially on the keys and casing. The overall design suggests it is a professional-grade instrument, possibly modeled after classic analog synthesizers like the Sequential Circuits Prophet series.
description: A vintage, worn electronic synthesizer keyboard with numerous control knobs, sliders, and a visible power cable. The casing is reddish-brown and heavily used, with black and white keys and multiple input/output ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keyboard, control panel with knobs and sliders, and the main interface for playing and adjusting sounds.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the keyboard, the angled control panel, and the attached power cable extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the input/output ports, power cable, and visible branding and labeling on the back panel.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side profile of the keys, the control panel's layout, and the cable trailing off to the side.
